decorative_elements.note: Late 19th century commercial building with stepped parapet front (false front concealing gable behind). Two pilaster-like raised sections at parapet crown. Entire facade painted dark grey/charcoal. Heritage character substantially obscured by paint and neglect.

condition_notes: Building appears vacant or semi-abandoned. Heavy graffiti/tagging. Diamond security grille over windows. Paint peeling. Stepped parapet crown still intact structurally. Significant heritage loss through paint and neglect.

condition_notes: Center building is a colourful painted renovation, reasonable condition. Flanking Victorian buildings show their age. Overall streetscape character is bohemian/eclectic Kensington Market style.

condition_notes: Functional parking structure with retail below. Brick piers in reasonable condition. Green mesh screens somewhat weathered. Commercial units active and maintained.

note: This is a vacant lot / demolition site, not a standing building. Shows a construction hoarding/fence covered in graffiti and a large blue tarp with graffiti. Behind the fence, bare trees and an open lot are visible. Adjacent buildings visible on right side (red brick, The Jerk Spot and bar). Tall condo/apartment building visible in background (white/grey, likely on adjacent block). Not suitable for individual building reconstruction.

decorative_elements.note: Vacant lot with construction hoarding covered in extensive graffiti art (tags, throw-ups). Blue tarp section with white graffiti. Exposed party wall of adjacent building visible on right edge, showing dark red/maroon painted brick.
